text.title: RICE for One-Person Cheatsheet Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules reweight RICE for the solo operator and bind the result to a WIP-1 weekly bet inside the Sunday roadmap ritual.

rule.statement: Reach is scored in absolute users or paying customers expected to be affected in the next 30 days, not as a normalised "% of MAU". At solo scale the relevant baselines are tens to a few hundred; using percentages collapses meaningful differences (5 users vs 50 users) into the same Reach=0.1 score.

rationale.source: RICE assumes a team's reach baseline; solo builders need adjusted weighting

rule.rationale: For a SaaS with 80 active users, "affects 20" is huge and "affects 2" is trivial — both look like 0.x in a percentage scheme. Absolute counts preserve the signal that drives a solo builder's revenue.

rule.statement: Impact is forced to a 4-step ladder tied to revenue or retention, not vibes: 3 = item is on the activation or conversion path of a paying segment; 2 = item materially improves retention of existing payers (churn, NPS); 1 = item improves operator efficiency or product polish; 0.25 = nice-to-have, no revenue path. Every Impact score in the backlog must cite the segment or metric that justifies its level.

rationale.source: Solo builder Impact must be revenue-anchored; vibes inflate everything to 2-3

rule.rationale: Without a forced ladder, the operator's own enthusiasm puts every item at Impact 3 and the ranking collapses. The revenue-or-retention anchor is the only thing that survives the solo founder's bias for the next shiny thing.

rule.statement: Confidence is collapsed to three discrete levels: H = 1.0 (you have direct evidence — paying users asked for this, or a measured drop-off pinpoints the cause), M = 0.6 (informed inference — analogous case studies, related but indirect evidence), L = 0.3 (guess — no evidence yet). Fractional self-grading ("70% confident") is forbidden; the operator must commit to one of the three.

rationale.source: Confidence at solo scale becomes vibes without discrete buckets

rule.rationale: "75% confident" is indistinguishable from "70%" and both are usually masked guesses. Three buckets force the operator to either find evidence (move to H) or admit it's a guess (drop to L), which changes the ranking.

rule.statement: Effort is scored in solo dev-days (8 focused hours = 1 day), not dev-weeks, and is multiplied by a context-switch tax of 1.3 if the item requires switching across more than two of: codebase, infra, marketing surface, customer support. The tax converts "looks like 2 days" into "actually 2.6 days" when the work crosses domains the solo operator owns alone.

rationale.source: Solo builder is the bottleneck; dev-weeks blur the single-person cost

rule.rationale: A team can parallelise across roles; a solo operator pays a real context-switch tax that dev-weeks hides. Scoring in days and surfacing the tax keeps the operator from cheerfully under-estimating cross-domain work.

rule.statement: WIP-1 framing: at the Sunday roadmap ritual the operator computes RICE = (Reach × Impact × Confidence) / Effort for every backlog item, ranks the list, and commits to the top-1 as the only bet of the week. Items 2-N are visible but not started. If the top-1 is blocked mid-week, the operator picks up item 2 — never both at once. Multi-bet weeks are explicitly forbidden.

rationale.source: Solo builder Sunday roadmap needs WIP-1 framing, not a stack of parallel work

rule.rationale: A ranked list without a WIP cap turns into a parallel-work fantasy; the solo operator starts three items and finishes none. WIP-1 converts the same ranking into actual shipping, which is the whole point.

example.title: Sunday ritual for an 80-user solo SaaS
example.code:
Backlog after solo-adjusted RICE:
Item Reach Impact Conf Effort RICE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Stripe failed-payment recovery 12 3 1.0 2.0d 18.0 Bulk export of invoices 6 2 1.0 1.0d 12.0 Marketing-site rewrite 80 1 0.6 5.2d 9.2 (1.3 tax — codebase+marketing) New onboarding video 3 2 0.3 1.5d 1.2 Refactor billing module 8 1 0.6 4.0d 1.2
WIP-1 commitment: Week-bet: Stripe failed-payment recovery (RICE 18.0) All other items deferred. If Stripe item blocks on a vendor support ticket, fall back to Bulk export — not both.
Justifications for audit: Reach 12: based on Stripe dashboard, 12 of 80 active users had a failed charge in the last 30d. Impact 3: payment-recovery is directly on the conversion-of-paying path. Confidence 1.0: H — three users emailed about it last week. Effort 2.0d: no context tax — single codebase, no marketing change.
example.note: The single weekly bet is now defensible against the operator's own enthusiasm; deferred items remain visible without splitting attention.
